method: The CO₂ mitigation metric is an indicative, assumption-based index — not a measured or attributable emission reduction. Each assumption entry maps a mitigation topic to (a) a relative score (0–20) reflecting the topic's global mitigation potential and how directly a policy/grant portfolio can act on it, and (b) the topic's order-of-magnitude global sectoral mitigation potential for 2030 in GtCO₂e per year, following the ranges reported in IPCC AR6 WGIII SPM.7 (options costing < USD 100/tCO₂e). A scenario matches an entry when any of its match terms appears in the scenario's topics or document texts; the scenario's index is the sum of matched scores, and the GtCO₂e/yr column sums the matched global ranges. Use it to compare scenarios relative to each other, never as a forecast of what the scenario itself would mitigate. Edit this file to change scores, ranges or match terms — the UI reads it directly.
basis_note: Ranges are rounded order-of-magnitude values based on IPCC AR6 WGIII (2022), Figure SPM.7: net global emission-reduction potential in 2030 at costs below USD 100/tCO₂e. Enabling topics (digitalisation, just transition, monitoring) carry a small score and a 0–0 range: they enable mitigation but have no separable sectoral potential.
method_cost_savings: Potential cost savings are an indicative €/year range for one typical industrial or regional actor, not a computed business case. Each driver maps regulatory/operational match terms to an order-of-magnitude annual saving: avoided EU ETS allowance and CBAM costs, avoided enforcement penalties for non-compliance, avoided waste-disposal and material costs, energy-bill savings, avoided input costs, and possible carbon-credit revenue. A scenario matches a driver when any of its match terms appears (whole-word) in the scenario's topics or document texts; the scenario's range sums the matched driver ranges. Real savings depend entirely on the actor's size, sector and baseline — treat these figures as a screening signal for which value pools a scenario touches.
method_uncertainty: All Stage-5 figures carry deliberate uncertainty bands. Funding: a Horizon call-topic budget usually funds several projects, so the realisable range for one consortium is shown as 25–100 % of the summed published budgets; grants without budget data add unquantified upside. CO₂ and cost savings: shown as the sum of matched low/high assumption ranges. Confidence rating per scenario: because every metric here rests on keyword matching and generic assumptions, the rating is capped at 75/100 — full certainty is not attainable at this screening level, and a perfect score would be misleading. Each scenario starts at 75 and is reduced for missing grant budget data (up to −40), a small document base (fewer than 3 documents −25, fewer than 6 −10), a CO₂ match consisting mostly of enabling rather than sectoral topics (−10), and documents that came from cached snapshot/sample data instead of the live EU endpoints (−5, possible staleness). ≥60 = High (meaning: as reliable as a keyword screening can be), ≥40 = Medium, below = Low. This is a data-completeness indicator, not a statistical confidence interval — for decisions, always verify against the linked source documents.

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: solar ~2–7, wind ~2.1–5.6 GtCO₂e/yr combined range, rounded.
example_tasks: commission a site solar/wind yield assessment with a grid-connection pre-study
tender a rooftop-PV or wind feasibility study including permitting screening
negotiate a power purchase agreement (PPA) or set up an energy community

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7 bioelectricity and biofuels, sustainability-constrained.
example_tasks: inventory available residue and feedstock streams (quantities, seasonality, moisture)
run a pre-feasibility study for a biogas/bioenergy unit including heat off-take mapping
secure feedstock letters of intent with local suppliers

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: reduced conversion + restoration of forests and other ecosystems.
example_tasks: map degraded parcels and agree restoration targets with land owners
establish a carbon-sink monitoring baseline (field plots + remote sensing)
pursue sustainable forest-management certification (e.g. FSC/PEFC)

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: soil carbon sequestration in croplands/grasslands, agroforestry, nutrient and rice management.
example_tasks: run a soil-carbon baseline measurement on representative parcels
pilot carbon-farming practices (cover crops, reduced tillage) on selected fields
set up monitoring/reporting (MRV) and register with a carbon-farming certification scheme

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7 industry: material efficiency, enhanced recycling and circular material flows.
example_tasks: map waste and by-product streams including volumes and gate fees
hold an industrial-symbiosis matchmaking workshop with neighbouring plants
pilot one recycled/secondary-material substitution in production

assumptions.basis: Substitution of fossil/carbon-intensive products and carbon storage in long-lived bio-based products; conservative literature-based range.
example_tasks: screen your product portfolio for fossil components replaceable by bio-based alternatives
run lab/pilot trials with bio-based material suppliers
commission a life-cycle assessment (LCA) to document the footprint advantage

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: CCS/BECCS/DACCS deployment potentials for 2030 are modest but scale later.
example_tasks: screen on-site CO₂ point sources (volume, concentration, purity)
identify storage or utilisation partners and feasible transport routes
commission a pre-FEED study for a capture pilot

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: demand-side efficiency in buildings and industry.
example_tasks: commission an energy audit (EN 16247) of buildings and processes
implement quick-win measures (heat recovery, insulation, controls)
prepare a renovation investment plan with financing options

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: fuel-efficient and electric vehicles, shift of transport modes, low-emission fuels.
example_tasks: establish a fleet and logistics emissions baseline
pilot electric or low-emission vehicles on selected routes
assess renewable-fuel supply options for heavy transport

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: reduced landfill methane, waste prevention and valorisation.
example_tasks: audit waste streams and landfill-diversion options
pilot separate collection and valorisation of biowaste
assess biogas capture from residual organic streams

assumptions.basis: IPCC AR6 SPM.7: fuel switching and hydrogen in industry, modest by 2030.
example_tasks: assess on-site hydrogen demand and supply options
join or initiate a regional hydrogen-valley partnership
pre-study electrolyser integration with renewable supply

assumptions.basis: Enabling topic: supports mitigation decisions (MRV, product passports) without separable sectoral potential.
example_tasks: set up FAIR data infrastructure for material and emission tracking
pilot a digital product passport for one product line

assumptions.basis: Enabling topic: fairness and acceptance condition the feasibility of all other options (IPCC AR6 WGIII ch. 17).
example_tasks: map affected stakeholders and set up a co-creation forum
design reskilling paths with regional training providers

cost_saving_assumptions.driver: Avoided EU ETS / CBAM carbon costs
cost_saving_assumptions.basis: EU allowances traded ~€60–90/tCO₂e in recent years; a mid-size industrial site emitting 10–100 ktCO₂e/yr avoids this per tonne abated, and CBAM extends carbon costs to imports.

National enforcement of EU environmental law (fines, remediation orders) plus penalty regimes in newer acts (e.g. ESPR, CSRD) that can reach percentages of turnover; EU infringement penalties on Member States cascade into national enforcement pressure.
Efficiency measures and on-site renewables typically cut 10–40 % of an industrial actor's energy bill.
Avoided landfill/incineration gate fees, lower virgin-material purchasing, revenue from valorised residues.
Nutrient recovery and improved soil management reduce mineral fertiliser purchases and input losses at farm/regional scale.
The EU Carbon Removals Certification Framework (Reg. (EU) 2024/3012) opens certified credit revenue for removals, carbon farming and storage in bio-based products.
